title: Stripe PaymentIntent creation and webhook order confirmation
description:
## Parent

merch-shop-BE-13e

## What to build

Two endpoints that together form the complete checkout flow:

1. POST /payments/payment-intent — authenticated Buyer passes their cartId, BE creates a Stripe PaymentIntent for the cart total in USD, returns the clientSecret to the frontend.

2. POST /payments/webhook — receives Stripe payment_intent.succeeded events. Verifies the webhook signature (mandatory — unsigned requests are rejected with 400). On success: creates an Order record, forwards line items to MockSupplierService, sends an order confirmation email via Resend, and deletes the Cart. The entire flow is idempotent: a duplicate webhook for the same PaymentIntent is a no-op.

The webhook endpoint receives a raw Buffer (registered before the global JSON parser in main.ts) so Stripe signature verification works correctly.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] `POST /api/payments/payment-intent` with a valid cartId returns `{ clientSecret: "..." }`
- [ ] PaymentIntent amount equals the sum of (SKU price × quantity) across all CartItems, in cents
- [ ] `POST /api/payments/webhook` with valid signature and `payment_intent.succeeded` creates an Order with status CONFIRMED
- [ ] Order is then updated to FORWARDED with a supplierReference after MockSupplierService.submitOrder resolves
- [ ] Order confirmation email is sent via Resend after forwarding
- [ ] Cart is deleted after Order is confirmed
- [ ] Second webhook with the same PaymentIntent ID is a no-op (Order not duplicated)
- [ ] Webhook with invalid signature returns 400 (no Order created)
- [ ] Integration test: mock Stripe constructEvent, verify Order created + Cart deleted
- [ ] Integration test: second webhook with same PaymentIntentId — only one Order exists
- [ ] Unit test: invalid signature rejected before any DB writes

## Blocked by

- merch-shop-BE-q9l

title: Account Cart and guest-to-account merge
description:
## Parent

merch-shop-BE-13e

## What to build

Authenticated Buyers get a persistent Cart tied to their Account.id (resolved via AccountsService.findOrCreate). Cart behavior is identical to the guest cart but keyed on accountId instead of sessionId and has no TTL. On login, the frontend calls POST /cart/merge with the guest sessionId to fold guest cart items into the account cart — duplicate SKUs keep the account cart's quantity, and the guest cart is deleted.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] `GET /api/cart` with a valid Clerk JWT returns (or creates) the Account's Cart
- [ ] `POST /api/cart/items` adds items to the Account Cart
- [ ] `DELETE /api/cart/items/:skuId` removes items from the Account Cart
- [ ] Account Cart has no expiresAt (null)
- [ ] `POST /api/cart/merge` with a valid `sessionId` body moves all guest cart items into the Account Cart
- [ ] Merging a SKU already in the Account Cart keeps the Account Cart's quantity (does not double-count)
- [ ] Guest Cart is deleted after a successful merge
- [ ] Merging a non-existent sessionId is a no-op (no error)
- [ ] Integration test: full account cart lifecycle
- [ ] Integration test: merge guest cart into account cart, verify guest cart gone and items present

## Blocked by

- merch-shop-BE-j0g
- merch-shop-BE-pzv

title: Admin catalog write API: CRUD for catalog entities and SKU availability
description:
## Parent

merch-shop-BE-13e

## What to build

Admin-only write endpoints for the full catalog: create/update/delete Publishers, Games, Teams, Characters, Products, and SKUs. Also includes the SKU availability toggle (PATCH /skus/:id/availability) which is the primary operational control for the drop-ship model. All endpoints require AdminGuard (Clerk JWT + Account.role === ADMIN in DB).

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] `POST /api/publishers` (AdminGuard) creates a Publisher; 403 for non-admin
- [ ] `PATCH /api/publishers/:id` updates a Publisher; 403 for non-admin
- [ ] `DELETE /api/publishers/:id` deletes a Publisher; 403 for non-admin
- [ ] Same pattern works for Games, Teams, Characters, Products
- [ ] `POST /api/skus` creates a SKU under a Product with price and attributes
- [ ] `PATCH /api/skus/:id/availability` flips the SKU's available boolean; 403 for non-admin
- [ ] AdminGuard returns 401 when no Bearer token is present
- [ ] AdminGuard returns 403 when token is valid but Account.role is BUYER
- [ ] Integration test: Admin creates Publisher → Game → Team → Product → SKU end-to-end
- [ ] Integration test: BUYER role rejected on all write endpoints

## Blocked by

- merch-shop-BE-giv
- merch-shop-BE-j0g

title: Guest Cart: UUID-cookie cart with availability check
description:
## Parent

merch-shop-BE-13e

## What to build

A server-side Cart for Guests, identified by a UUID stored in an HttpOnly cookie. Guests can view their Cart, add SKUs, update quantities, and remove items — all without authenticating. The Cart persists in PostgreSQL with accountId=null and an expiresAt TTL. Adding an unavailable SKU is rejected with 400.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] `GET /api/cart` with no auth and no cookie creates a new guest Cart and sets a `cart_session` HttpOnly cookie
- [ ] `GET /api/cart` with an existing `cart_session` cookie returns the same Cart
- [ ] `POST /api/cart/items` with a valid available SKU adds the item (or updates quantity if already present)
- [ ] `POST /api/cart/items` with an unavailable SKU returns 400
- [ ] `POST /api/cart/items` with a non-existent SKU returns 404
- [ ] `DELETE /api/cart/items/:skuId` removes the item from the Cart
- [ ] Cart rows with accountId=null have an expiresAt set to GUEST_CART_TTL_DAYS days from creation
- [ ] Integration tests: full guest cart lifecycle (create → add → update quantity → remove)
- [ ] Integration test: unavailable SKU rejected before Cart is modified

## Blocked by

- merch-shop-BE-81u

title: Account provisioning: upsert Account on first auth
description:
## Parent
merch-shop-BE-13e

## What to build

Two things in one vertical slice:

**1. AccountModule + AccountService seam (arch prefactor)**
Account is a first-class domain concept with no module. AdminGuard performs a raw Prisma query to check account role — bypassing any seam. Create AccountModule with AccountService exposing: `hasRole(accountId, role)`, `upsertFromClerk(clerkUser)`, `findById(accountId)`. AdminGuard calls `AccountService.hasRole()` — no direct Prisma in the guard.

**2. Account provisioning on first auth (feature)**
On the first request with a valid Clerk JWT, upsert an Account record linked to the Clerk user ID. Subsequent requests return the existing Account. A `GET /api/account/me` endpoint returns the current Account.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] AccountModule exports AccountService
- [ ] AccountService.upsertFromClerk() creates or updates Account on Clerk JWT validation
- [ ] AccountService.hasRole(id, role) used by AdminGuard — no direct Prisma in guard
- [ ] `GET /api/account/me` returns current Account (401 if unauthenticated)
- [ ] Integration test: first request with valid Clerk JWT creates Account; second request returns same Account
- [ ] App compiles and existing integration tests pass

## Blocked by

merch-shop-BE-772 — Prefactor: surface PrismaModule coupling in module declarations

title: Catalog read API: public browse + facet filtering
description:
## Parent

merch-shop-BE-13e

## What to build

Public GET endpoints that let Buyers browse the full catalog and filter Products by orthogonal facets. No authentication required. All six catalog resources (Publisher, Game, Team, Character, Product, SKU) are readable. Products support combined facet filtering via query params — any combination of gameId, teamId, and characterId is valid.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] `GET /api/publishers` returns all Publishers ordered by name
- [ ] `GET /api/publishers/:slug` returns a single Publisher or 404
- [ ] `GET /api/games` returns all Games; `?publisherId=` filters by Publisher
- [ ] `GET /api/games/:slug` returns a single Game with its Publisher
- [ ] `GET /api/teams` returns all Teams; `?gameId=` filters by Game
- [ ] `GET /api/characters` returns all Characters; `?gameId=` filters by Game
- [ ] `GET /api/products` returns all Products with their available SKUs and facet relations
- [ ] `GET /api/products?gameId=X` returns only Products with that Game facet
- [ ] `GET /api/products?gameId=X&teamId=Y` returns Products matching both facets
- [ ] `GET /api/products/:id` returns a single Product with full SKU list and facet relations
- [ ] `GET /api/skus?productId=X` returns all SKUs for a Product
- [ ] Integration tests cover ProductsService.findAll with no filters, single facet, and combined facets
- [ ] Integration tests confirm unavailable SKUs are excluded from the findAll SKU list

## Blocked by

- merch-shop-BE-81u

title: Project bootstrap: install, migrate, start
description:
## Parent

merch-shop-BE-13e

## What to build

Get the project running end-to-end locally so every subsequent slice has a working baseline. This means: dependencies installed, Postgres running via Docker Compose, Prisma migrations applied, app starts without errors, and Swagger UI is reachable.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] `npm install` completes without errors
- [ ] `.env` created from `.env.example` with valid local values (Postgres credentials, stub keys for Clerk/Stripe/Resend)
- [ ] `docker compose up db` starts Postgres on port 5432
- [ ] `npx prisma migrate dev --name init` applies the full schema with no errors
- [ ] `npm run start:dev` starts the app without runtime errors
- [ ] `GET /api/docs` returns 200 and Swagger UI renders all routes
- [ ] `GET /api/publishers` returns an empty array (not a 500)

## Blocked by

None — can start immediately.
close_reason: App starts, migrations applied, GET /api/publishers → 200 [], GET /api/docs → 200. Two TS type fixes in skus.service and payments.service.

title: E-Sport Merch Shop Backend — Full MVP Build
description:
## Problem Statement

Buyers wanting to purchase licensed e-sport merchandise have no backend to serve catalog data, manage their cart, or process orders. Without a backend, the storefront cannot display products filtered by Game, Team, or Character facets; cannot persist a Buyer's Cart across sessions; and cannot accept payment or forward confirmed Orders to a Supplier for fulfillment.

## Solution

A NestJS REST API backed by PostgreSQL (via Prisma) that:
- Serves a multi-publisher product catalog filterable by orthogonal facets (Game, Team, Character)
- Persists Carts server-side for both Guests (UUID cookie) and authenticated Accounts (Clerk JWT)
- Accepts Stripe payments via a webhook-driven flow that confirms Orders and forwards them to a Supplier
- Sends order confirmation emails via Resend
- Exposes an Admin API (role-gated) for catalog and SKU management

## User Stories

1. As a Buyer, I want to browse all Products so that I can discover merch across all publishers and games.
2. As a Buyer, I want to filter Products by Game so that I can see only merch for a specific game I follow.
3. As a Buyer, I want to filter Products by Team so that I can find merch for my favourite esport team.
4. As a Buyer, I want to filter Products by Character so that I can browse merch for a specific in-game figure.
5. As a Buyer, I want to combine facet filters (e.g. Game + Team) so that I can narrow results to exactly what I want.
6. As a Buyer, I want to view a Product's detail page including all available SKUs so that I can choose the right size, colour, or variant.
7. As a Guest, I want to add a SKU to my Cart without logging in so that I can shop without creating an Account.
8. As a Guest, I want my Cart to persist across page reloads and tab closes so that I do not lose my selections.
9. As a Guest, I want to update quantities or remove items from my Cart so that I can adjust my purchase before checkout.
10. As a Guest, I want to complete checkout and place an Order with just my email so that I do not need an Account to buy.
11. As an Account holder, I want my Cart to persist across devices so that I can start shopping on one device and finish on another.
12. As an Account holder, I want my Guest Cart to merge into my Account Cart when I log in so that I do not lose items I added before signing in.
13. As an Account holder, I want to initiate payment for my Cart and receive a Stripe client secret so that I can complete payment securely on the frontend.
14. As a Buyer, I want to receive an order confirmation email after successful payment so that I have a record of my purchase.
15. As a Buyer, I want to view my Order history so that I can track past purchases.
16. As an Admin, I want to create and update Publishers so that I can onboard new game companies to the platform.
17. As an Admin, I want to create and update Games under a Publisher so that I can expand the catalog to new esport titles.
18. As an Admin, I want to create and update Teams under a Game so that I can manage competing organisations on the platform.
19. As an Admin, I want to create and update Characters under a Game so that I can add in-game figures as product facets.
20. As an Admin, I want to create Products and assign them any combination of Game, Team, and Character facets so that I can accurately tag merch that crosses multiple dimensions.
21. As an Admin, I want to create SKUs under a Product with a price and attributes (size, colour, etc.) so that I can offer variants at correct prices.
22. As an Admin, I want to flip a SKU's Availability flag so that I can remove unavailable variants from the Buyer's cart and catalog without deleting them.
23. As an Admin, I want to view all Orders so that I can monitor purchases and handle support queries.
24. As the system, I want to verify Stripe webhook signatures so that I only process legitimate payment events.
25. As the system, I want to create an Order idempotently on payment_intent.succeeded so that retried webhooks do not produce duplicate Orders.
26. As the system, I want to forward a confirmed Order to the Supplier so that physical goods are dispatched to the Buyer.
27. As the system, I want to delete the Cart after an Order is confirmed so that stale cart data does not accumulate.
28. As the system, I want to rate-limit checkout and auth endpoints so that abuse and card-testing attacks are mitigated.

## Implementation Decisions

- **Framework**: NestJS with TypeScript. Modules map directly to domain boundaries: Catalog, Commerce, Fulfillment, Payments, Notifications, Auth.

- **Database**: PostgreSQL via Prisma ORM. Schema uses a Publisher → Game → Team/Character hierarchy for catalog entities. Products carry nullable foreign keys to Game, Team, and Character — any combination is valid (orthogonal facets, per ADR-0002).

- **Auth**: Authentication is fully delegated to Clerk (per ADR-0004). The BE exposes no login or registration endpoints. A ClerkGuard verifies the Bearer JWT on protected routes using Clerk's JWKS. An AdminGuard extends ClerkGuard with a DB lookup to confirm the Account has role ADMIN.

- **Guest Cart**: Guest Carts are stored in PostgreSQL with accountId=null, identified by a UUID in an HttpOnly cookie (per ADR-0006). Cart rows with null accountId carry an expiresAt TTL. On Account login, the frontend calls POST /cart/merge with the guest sessionId to merge guest items into the account Cart.

- **Payments**: Stripe is the payment gateway (per ADR-0005). The frontend creates a PaymentIntent via POST /payments/payment-intent (auth required). The order confirmation flow is entirely webhook-driven: payment_intent.succeeded → confirm Order → forward to Supplier → send email → delete Cart. Webhook signature verification is mandatory. The flow is idempotent: duplicate webhooks for the same PaymentIntent are no-ops.

- **Supplier**: All fulfillment is routed through a SupplierPort interface (submitOrder, checkAvailability). The concrete implementation for MVP is MockSupplierService which logs and returns a synthetic reference. Swapping to a real Supplier requires implementing the port — no other code changes.

- **Notifications**: Resend sends a single transactional email (order confirmation) per Order. The email is triggered inside the Stripe webhook handler after Supplier forwarding succeeds.

- **Pricing**: All prices stored and transacted in USD as a Decimal(10,2). No per-currency columns exist (per ADR-0008). Stripe handles local currency display on the FE.

- **Security**: Helmet applied globally. ThrottlerModule configured with three rate-limit tiers: default (100 req/min), auth (10 req/min), checkout (5 req/min). CORS locked to FRONTEND_URL env var. Stripe webhook endpoint receives raw body before the global JSON parser.

- **Admin surface**: Admin endpoints (POST/PATCH/DELETE on catalog, GET /orders) are gated by AdminGuard. No admin UI is built; Admin interacts via REST API directly or through a future admin panel.

- **Deployment**: Docker Compose for local dev (app + postgres services). docker-compose.test.yml provides an ephemeral test DB on port 5433 using tmpfs (fast, no persistent volume).

## Testing Decisions

- **What makes a good test**: Test the service layer directly — not HTTP handlers, not Prisma internals. Assert on the return value and on follow-up DB state, not on which Prisma methods were called.

- **Unit tests** (Jest, `*.spec.ts`): Service methods with Prisma mocked via jest.fn(). Cover business rules: unavailable SKU rejected in addItem, idempotency check in confirmOrder, AdminGuard rejects non-ADMIN roles.

- **Integration tests** (Jest, `*.integration.spec.ts`, `jest.integration.config.js`): Services injected with a real PrismaService pointed at the test DB (PostgreSQL on port 5433, migrated via globalSetup). Cover: full cart lifecycle (create → add → remove → merge), full order confirmation flow (PaymentIntent → webhook → Order created + Supplier called + Cart deleted).

- **Supplier port in tests**: Integration tests inject MockSupplierService for the SUPPLIER_PORT token. No real Supplier API calls in any test environment.

- **No E2E (HTTP) tests for MVP**: The service layer is the highest shared seam. Adding HTTP-layer tests would duplicate coverage without catching new failure modes given thin controllers.

## Out of Scope

- Real Supplier integration (Printful or other) — MockSupplierService only until a Supplier contract exists
- Inventory management, stock counts, low-stock warnings, pre-order queues — Availability is a boolean flag only (per ADR-0003)
- Multi-currency pricing — USD only for MVP (per ADR-0008)
- Order cancellation, returns, or refund flows
- Admin UI — API endpoints only; UI is a separate project
- Supplier-facing portal or webhook receiver
- Search (full-text or fuzzy) — facet filtering via query params only
- Product reviews or ratings
- Discount codes, promotions, or vouchers
- Saved shipping addresses on Account

## Further Notes

- The Stripe webhook handler must be registered BEFORE the global JSON body parser in main.ts so that express.raw() receives the raw Buffer needed for signature verification.
- Guest Cart TTL is configured via GUEST_CART_TTL_DAYS env var (default 7). A periodic cleanup job to prune expired carts is out of scope for MVP but should be added before production launch.
- The MockSupplierService logs forwarded orders at INFO level — sufficient for development tracing without a real Supplier API.
- ClerkUserId (from JWT sub claim) is used as the accountId reference throughout the system — no separate internal user ID is issued.

title: Scaffold NestJS project structure
description: Initialize the full NestJS backend project for the merch shop. Includes: package.json, Prisma schema, Docker Compose, all domain modules (catalog, commerce, fulfillment, payments, notifications), Clerk guard, Stripe webhook, Resend email port.
close_reason: Full NestJS scaffold written: 45 source files, Prisma schema, Docker Compose, all domain modules

title: Order history and security verification
description:
## Parent

merch-shop-BE-13e

## What to build

Two concerns completed together since both are thin and test the same running app:

**Order history**: authenticated Buyers can list their own Orders; Admins can list all Orders.

**Security verification**: confirm that Helmet security headers are present on all responses, ThrottlerModule fires 429 on burst traffic to rate-limited endpoints, and CORS rejects requests from origins not matching FRONTEND_URL.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] `GET /api/orders/mine` (ClerkGuard) returns the authenticated Account's Orders with line items
- [ ] `GET /api/orders/mine` returns empty array (not 404) when Account has no Orders
- [ ] `GET /api/orders` (AdminGuard) returns all Orders; 403 for non-admin
- [ ] `GET /api/orders/:id` (ClerkGuard) returns a single Order with line items
- [ ] Integration test: Buyer can retrieve their own confirmed Order after the webhook flow
- [ ] Integration test: Admin sees all Orders; Buyer sees only their own
- [ ] Smoke test: response headers include `X-Content-Type-Options`, `X-Frame-Options` (Helmet present)
- [ ] Smoke test: 11 rapid requests to a throttled endpoint returns at least one 429
- [ ] Smoke test: request with `Origin: http://evil.example.com` is rejected by CORS (missing or blocked CORS headers)

## Blocked by

- merch-shop-BE-87f

title: Refactor: deepen Order intake into OrdersService
description:
## Parent
merch-shop-BE-13e

## What to build

PaymentsService.confirmOrder (68 lines) spans four domain concerns — cart read, order creation, fulfillment dispatch, notification — with zero locality. OrdersService has no create method; order intake is untestable without Stripe.

Add `OrdersService.confirm(intent: ConfirmedPaymentIntent)` that contains all logic currently in PaymentsService.confirmOrder: idempotency check, cart read, Order creation (via Prisma), CartService.clearCart(), supplier dispatch via SUPPLIER_PORT, notification via NOTIFICATION_PORT, and order status update. PaymentsService.handleWebhook calls `orders.confirm()` and knows nothing else about order creation.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] OrdersService.confirm() is the single place where Orders are created
- [ ] PaymentsService.confirmOrder is deleted; replaced by a single orders.confirm() call
- [ ] CartService.clearCart() is called from OrdersService (not bypassed via direct Prisma)
- [ ] OrdersService injects NOTIFICATION_PORT, not the concrete NotificationsService
- [ ] Integration test: given a ConfirmedPaymentIntent, confirm() creates Order, clears Cart, calls supplier and notification ports — no Stripe client required
- [ ] Existing integration tests pass

## Blocked by

- merch-shop-BE-j0g — Account provisioning: upsert Account on first auth
- merch-shop-BE-bd2 — Prefactor: introduce NOTIFICATION_PORT seam

title: Prefactor: export a read interface from CatalogModule
description:
## Parent
merch-shop-BE-13e

## What to build

CatalogModule currently exports nothing. Other modules (Payments computing cart total, Commerce checking SKU availability) bypass CatalogModule and hit Prisma directly. No seam exists for catalog reads — schema changes in the catalog ripple freely into other modules.

Export a small read facade from CatalogModule: `findProducts(filter)`, `getSkuPrice(skuId)`, `findPublisher(slug)`. The 6 internal services remain as implementation details. PaymentsService and CommerceModule use the exported interface; they declare `CatalogModule` in their `imports` array.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] CatalogModule exports a read interface (facade or service) with `findProducts`, `getSkuPrice`, `findPublisher`
- [ ] PaymentsService.createPaymentIntent uses `CatalogModule.getSkuPrice` not raw Prisma for SKU price
- [ ] CommerceModule declares `CatalogModule` in its `imports`
- [ ] Integration tests pass

## Blocked by

merch-shop-BE-772 — Prefactor: surface PrismaModule coupling in module declarations

title: Prefactor: introduce NOTIFICATION_PORT seam
description:
## Parent
merch-shop-BE-13e

## What to build

PaymentsService imports NotificationsService as a concrete class. No seam — the Resend adapter is welded to the payment path and integration tests require a live Resend connection.

Mirror the existing `SUPPLIER_PORT` pattern. Create a `NOTIFICATION_PORT` injection token and `NotificationPort` interface with a single `sendOrderConfirmation(data)` method. `ResendAdapter` implements it in prod. A `LogNotificationAdapter` (no-op) is registered for integration tests. PaymentsService injects `NOTIFICATION_PORT` via `@Inject(NOTIFICATION_PORT)` — no longer importing the concrete class.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] `NOTIFICATION_PORT` symbol and `NotificationPort` interface exist in the notifications module
- [ ] `ResendAdapter` is the prod implementation wired in NotificationsModule
- [ ] `LogNotificationAdapter` registered for integration test context
- [ ] PaymentsService injects `NOTIFICATION_PORT`, not the concrete `NotificationsService`
- [ ] Integration tests run without a Resend API key

## Blocked by

None — can start immediately.

title: Prefactor: extract cart identity resolution to CartSessionContext
description:
## Parent
merch-shop-BE-13e

## What to build

`CartController.resolveSession()` — deciding whether a request is guest or authenticated — is domain logic duplicated across 3 controller methods inside the HTTP layer. Extract it.

Create a NestJS param decorator `@CartSession()` that returns a `CartSessionContext` value object `{ type: 'guest' | 'account', id: string }`. Remove the `resolveSession()` private method from CartController. All three handler methods (get, addItem, removeItem) receive `CartSessionContext` as a typed parameter. Update `CartService.getOrCreate` to accept it.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] `@CartSession()` decorator extracts identity from request (cookie `sessionId` or Clerk JWT `accountId`)
- [ ] CartController has no `resolveSession()` method
- [ ] CartService.getOrCreate accepts CartSessionContext
- [ ] Integration tests for guest cart and account cart pass

## Blocked by

None — can start immediately.

title: Prefactor: surface PrismaModule coupling in module declarations
description:
## Parent
merch-shop-BE-13e

## What to build

Remove `@Global()` from PrismaModule so that each module's DB dependency is visible in its own `imports` array. Currently 10 service files inject PrismaService but zero modules declare it — the module graph is misleading.

Add `PrismaModule` to `imports` in CatalogModule, CommerceModule, PaymentsModule, and AuthModule. No logic changes — purely structural.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] PrismaModule has no `@Global()` decorator
- [ ] CatalogModule, CommerceModule, PaymentsModule, AuthModule each declare PrismaModule in imports
- [ ] App compiles without errors
- [ ] Integration tests pass

## Blocked by

None — can start immediately.
